title: Timeless Storage Bed with 12 Drawers
summary: A full storage bed: plywood headboard/footboard panels and two 12-drawer storage bases flanking the mattress — real casework, real drawer slides, no wasted space under the bed.
description:
This is two separate builds joined into one bed: a headboard/footboard pair, and two long storage bases (one per side of the mattress) that carry six drawers each. Treat the storage bases like cabinets on their sides — because that's what they are — and the drawer-slide layout is the part that decides whether the whole thing feels right or fights you every day.

Install the drawer slides before you box in the dividers with plywood, not after. Once the back panel is on and the box is closed up, you're reaching in blind. Inset the slides 3/4" from the front edge of the opening so there's room for a drawer face to sit proud later, and cut every drawer box exactly 1" narrower than its opening — that's the clearance most bottom-mount slides need, and it's unforgiving in both directions: too tight and the drawer binds, too loose and it racks.

The headboard and footboard are the same panel with different leg lengths, so build them side by side and you'll catch mismatches before they're permanent. The moulding detail on the front is optional — skip it for a plainer look, or add it for a piece that reads as furniture instead of a box on legs.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood storage-base sides down to consistent width.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet — ripped into strips
materials.note: Ripped into ~15-3/4"-wide strips for the two storage-base sides and dividers.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood or backerboard, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Storage-base backs — also what squares the boxes up.

materials.name: Pine, 1x3, 8 ft
materials.note: Two for headboard/footboard trim, two for mattress slats.

materials.name: Decorative moulding, 8 ft
materials.note: Optional front detail on the headboard/footboard.

cutList.note: Same panel as the headboard, just shorter legs.
Optional. Long point to long point, both ends mitered 45°, not parallel.

steps.title: Build the headboard and footboard panels
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes around the top and side edges of both plywood panels. Attach the top and bottom trim to the front face, then attach the legs flush to the trim and to the back of the plywood with 1-1/4" pocket screws. The headboard and footboard are the same panel — the footboard just gets the shorter legs, so build both side by side and check them against each other before moving on.

steps.title: Add optional moulding detail
steps.body: If you're adding the moulding, glue and 3/4" brad-nail it to the panel faces now, mitered corners tight. Skip this step entirely for a plainer look — it changes nothing structural.

steps.title: Install drawer slides before boxing in the dividers
steps.body: This is the step to get right before anything else in the storage bases: attach drawer slides to both ends of each opening and to both sides of every center divider, inset 3/4" from the front edge so a drawer face has somewhere to sit. Do this now, while everything is still open and you can reach every screw — once the plywood back goes on, you can't.

steps.title: Assemble the storage-base frames and dividers
steps.body: Build the 2x4 frame for each storage base, then attach the plywood sides, ends, and dividers with pocket holes or 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Nail the 1/4" plywood back on last with 3/4" brad nails — beyond closing up the box, the back panel is what squares the whole base up, so check for square right before it goes on and hold it there while you nail.

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes
steps.body: Assemble each drawer box from 1x4 sides, fronts, and backs with 1-1/4" brad nails, then nail on the 1/4" plywood bottom with 3/4" brad nails. Every drawer box must be exactly 1" narrower than its opening — measure your actual openings before cutting, don't trust the plan numbers blindly, since the slides you bought set the real clearance.

steps.title: Hang the drawers and attach faces
steps.body: Attach the drawer-side slide members to each drawer box and slide the boxes into the storage bases. Attach the drawer faces to the boxes with an even gap around all sides — use spacers to hold the gap consistent while the glue sets. Add moulding to the faces here if you're using it.

steps.title: Build the drawer support boxes
steps.body: The storage bases carry real weight once loaded, so build a support box under each one flush to the back and to the headboard side — these are what keep the drawers from sagging the frame over time.

steps.title: Assemble the bed
steps.body: Screw the headboard and footboard to the storage bases. Add 2x2 cleats along the inside of the bases and lay 1x2 or 1x3 slats across them to support the mattress, screwed down so they can't shift. Check the whole assembly for square before you call it done.

images.alt: A platform bed with twelve storage drawers built into two side bases flanking the mattress
